steps.title: Identify the preservation gap
steps.content: The passage says some expressions in Indigenous languages appear only in songs, but language-preservation efforts have usually focused on spoken language. That means spoken-language preservation alone may miss information carried in songs.

steps.title: Close the gap
steps.content: A studies musical similarities, B merely notes how songs are composed, and D makes an unsupported comparison about types of ecological expressions. To preserve the comprehensive range of information, efforts must include songs. The answer is C.
